title: Online Gift Card Policies
description: What are the legal and privacy implications of using electronic gift cards across multiple retailers?
narrative: The user is trying to evaluate the potential risks and compliance requirements when purchasing or redeeming electronic gift cards from various online retailers, seeking information on privacy policies, data protection, and regulatory compliance relevant to international usage. Relevance is determined by documents that detail the legal framework, privacy notices, and cross-border considerations related to electronic gift cards.

title: Urban Mobility Sustainability
description: What are the most effective policy interventions that cities can implement to promote electric public transit and reduce carbon emissions?
narrative: The user seeks evidence-based strategies tailored to mid-sized European cities, prioritizing feasibility, cost-effectiveness, and social equity, with a focus on case studies, pilot projects, and measurable outcomes.

title: Social Media and Diet
description: How does social media influence dietary choices among adolescents?
narrative: The user wants research that demonstrates **causal mechanisms** (e.g., exposure to food advertising, peer influence, or algorithmic recommendations) rather than mere correlations. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed studies, studies employing longitudinal or experimental designs, and content that discusses policy or intervention implications for improving youth nutrition.

title: Hybrid Solar Resilience
description: How can photovoltaic modules be engineered to sustain optimal performance amid rapidly changing loads and environmental conditions?
narrative: The user is investigating design strategies for photovoltaic modules that maintain high efficiency and reliability when operating under variable electrical loads and fluctuating environmental factors such as irradiance, temperature, and wind. Relevant documents should cover adaptive control architectures, dynamic bypass switching, real‑time monitoring and fault‑tolerant design, as well as material or structural innovations that allow modules to respond rapidly to transient conditions without significant power loss. Exclude general solar energy reviews, market analyses, or unrelated engineering references that do not address performance resilience in variable‑load contexts.

title: Southeast Asia Digital Nomad Visas
description: What are the best long‑term visa options for digital nomads looking to work remotely in Southeast Asia?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on visa categories, application procedures, costs, and restrictions for digital nomads planning to stay in Southeast Asian countries for extended periods, with an emphasis on practical legal guidance and potential pitfalls.

title: Accessible Family Travel
description: What are the best vacation destinations that accommodate families with special needs?
narrative: The user is planning a vacation for a family that includes members with various disabilities. They need detailed information about destinations that provide full accessibility, such as wheelchair‑accessible attractions, specialized transportation, inclusive accommodations, medical support, and sensory‑friendly activities. Relevance is judged by how comprehensively a document covers accessible facilities, accessibility ratings, first‑hand reviews, and practical travel tips for families with special needs.

title: AI Ethics Governance
description: What frameworks best guide ethical AI deployment in healthcare?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive models and policies that balance patient privacy, algorithmic fairness, and regulatory compliance. Relevant documents should detail governance structures, stakeholder engagement practices, and case studies of AI in medical settings.

title: Biodegradable Food Packaging
description: Which biodegradable polymers have emerged as viable replacements for conventional plastics in food packaging over the last five years?
narrative: The user is a packaging engineer looking for up‑to‑date research on materials that can reduce environmental impact while preserving food safety and shelf life. Relevance criteria include peer‑reviewed journal articles, industry white papers, and patent filings published between 2019 and 2024 that detail material composition, mechanical and barrier properties, lifecycle assessments, regulatory approvals, and real‑world case studies of deployment in food products.

title: EU Shipping Emissions
description: What are the most recent EU regulations that govern greenhouse gas emissions for shipping operations?
narrative: The user is a maritime compliance officer seeking detailed, current legislative text and guidance notes on EU rules that apply to shipping vessels. Relevant documents must contain the specific regulatory acts, dates of enforcement, compliance thresholds, and any transitional provisions.

title: Insurance Policy Comparison
description: How do major insurance providers differ in coverage, deductibles, and premium costs for home and auto policies in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks a side‑by‑side evaluation of prominent insurers, emphasizing coverage limits, optional riders, deductible options, and current premium rates for dual home and auto coverage. Relevance criteria include comprehensive coverage details, clear cost comparisons, up‑to‑date price data, and evidence of policy terms such as claim handling and exclusions.

title: Green roof economics
description: What are the cost‑benefit outcomes of installing green roofs on commercial office buildings in temperate climates?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed economic analyses—peer‑reviewed studies, case reports, and financial models—focused on commercial office buildings (regional and national scope) that have implemented green roofs. Relevance criteria include documented initial installation and maintenance costs, quantifiable energy savings or utility bill reductions, building life‑extension metrics, and any reported environmental or tax incentive data. Studies should be from the last 15 years and specific to temperate climate zones (e.g., mid‑Northern Europe, the U.S. Midwest).

title: Late-Season Potato Planting
description: What are effective methods and timing for planting potatoes late in the season without compromising yields?
narrative: The user needs detailed guidance on late-season potato cultivation, including climate‑based planting dates, suitable variety selection for short crop cycles, preparatory steps such as pre‑germination, soil and moisture management, and post‑plant care like hill‑forming and frost protection. Relevance criteria: sources should provide practical, up‑to‑date recommendations, regional variations, and comparisons of techniques, but can exclude generic horticulture overviews that do not address late‑season specifics.

title: Remote Work Health
description: What are the long-term effects of remote work on employees' mental health and productivity?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly studies, systematic reviews, or policy reports that examine how sustained remote work arrangements influence mental well‑being, stress, work‑life balance, and productivity outcomes. Relevance is limited to evidence‑based research published in peer‑reviewed journals or reputable industry reports that provide clear methodology and measurable results, and excludes anecdotal blogs or personal opinion pieces.

title: ML Diagnostics
description: What recent peer‑reviewed studies compare CNNs and transformers for reducing false positives in mammography?
narrative: The user is a radiologist seeking evidence‑based evaluation of deep‑learning models for breast cancer screening. Relevance criteria: peer‑reviewed publications from the last five years that provide quantitative performance metrics (AUC, sensitivity, specificity) and compare convolutional neural networks with transformer‑based architectures for mammographic image analysis.

title: Digital Divide & Migration
description: How can digital platforms be leveraged to close the educational gap faced by migrant students in host countries?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based strategies, case studies, and policy recommendations that address technology access, language support, and teacher training to improve educational outcomes for migrant learners.

title: Sustainable Seattle cafés
description: Which environmentally certified coffee shops in Seattle offer artisanal roasting and local pastries?
narrative: The user wants cafés that hold third‑party green certifications, use artisanal roasting techniques, and provide pastries sourced from local producers in Seattle.

title: Urban Green Roofs
description: What are the best practices for designing and maintaining green roofs in dense city environments?
narrative: The user seeks detailed, practical guidance on the design, materials, maintenance schedules, cost-benefit analysis, and regulatory considerations for implementing green roof systems in high-density urban settings.

title: Climate Change Adaptation
description: What are the most effective climate change adaptation strategies for smallholder farms in the Sahel region?
narrative: The user wants actionable, field‑level adaptation measures specifically tailored to smallholder agriculture in the semi‑arid Sahel. Relevant documents should describe on‑farm interventions such as drought‑tolerant crop varieties, improved irrigation, soil and water conservation techniques, locally appropriate policy supports, and evidence of successful implementation in similar contexts. Non‑technical policy reviews, studies on other regions, or purely theoretical models are less relevant.

title: Remote Work Urban Planning
description: What effects does the increased prevalence of remote work have on city infrastructure, public transit usage, and urban planning decisions?
narrative: The user seeks detailed insights into how widespread remote work changes transportation demand, residential patterns, and municipal service allocation, requiring evidence from recent studies, case studies, and policy analyses.

title: Microplastics Seafood Risks
description: What health risks are posed by eating seafood containing microplastics, and how do exposure levels vary by species and geographic region?
narrative: The user is a public‑health researcher seeking the latest evidence on toxicological effects, bioaccumulation pathways, epidemiological findings, and regulatory guidance related to microplastic contamination in seafood. Relevant documents should provide species‑specific contamination data, measurement methods, exposure assessments, health impact studies, and geographic analysis to support risk assessment for vulnerable populations.

title: Policy Impact Analysis
description: How have recent EU renewable energy directives influenced deployment rates in small member states?
narrative: The user seeks recent, data‑driven evaluations of EU policy impacts on renewable energy infrastructure, specifically focusing on small member states; relevant documents must contain up‑to‑date studies, comparative deployment statistics, and credible policy analysis reporting the effects of the directives.

title: Elderly Remote Care Models
description: Which innovative remote monitoring strategies effectively improve health outcomes for elderly patients in rural areas?
narrative: The user is a rural healthcare administrator looking for evidence‑based remote monitoring interventions for older adults that reduce hospitalizations and improve quality of life. Relevance requires descriptions of specific programs (telehealth, wearable sensors, home telemetry), study designs, measured outcomes for the elderly, cost‑benefit analyses, and implementation lessons learned in rural settings.

title: Remote Work Effectiveness
description: What peer‑reviewed research studies from 2015–2025 provide quantitative evidence on how flexible remote work schedules influence employee well‑being?
narrative: The user wants to evaluate the real impact of remote‑work flexibility on employee well‑being. Relevance requires studies published in recognized academic venues (journals or major conferences), those that employ longitudinal or experimental designs, present explicit quantitative metrics (e.g., effect sizes, confidence intervals), and discuss policy or organizational implications. Non‑academic blog posts, opinion pieces, or studies lacking rigorous data are to be ignored.

title: Electric Aviation Feasibility
description: What are the technical, economic, and regulatory challenges and opportunities for adopting electric aircraft in commercial aviation?
narrative: The user is researching the feasibility of electric planes for future airline operations, focusing on battery technology limitations, cost implications, regulatory hurdles, and environmental benefits. Relevance requires discussion of battery chemistries, flight range, charging infrastructure, maintenance impacts, cost models, certifications, and policy incentives.

title: AI Ethics in Healthcare
description: What are the main ethical concerns and proposed guidelines for using AI-driven decision support systems in clinical settings?
narrative: A researcher is looking for current discussions and frameworks that address patient privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, and transparency in AI tools employed for diagnostics and treatment planning.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What are the most effective urban heat mitigation strategies and their impact on local biodiversity in major cities?
narrative: The user is a city planner seeking evidence-based mitigation measures that reduce urban heat islands while preserving or enhancing biodiversity. Relevant documents should discuss empirical studies or planning reports from major metropolitan areas, detail mechanisms of action, quantify temperature reductions, include biodiversity metrics, and recommend actionable interventions with cost or feasibility assessments.

title: Imbalanced ML Evaluation
description: How can I properly evaluate machine learning models on imbalanced datasets?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed methods to address class imbalance during model evaluation, including resampling strategies (oversampling, undersampling), cost‑sensitive learning, appropriate performance metrics (F1‑score, balanced accuracy, AUC‑ROC, PR curves), and validation techniques (stratified k‑fold, repeated cross‑validation). Relevant content should provide actionable guidance, best practice references, and examples from reputable research; purely descriptive or unrelated ML topics are not relevant.

title: COVID Vaccine Myth Busting
description: Which authoritative sources provide reliable evidence that debunks common myths about COVID‑19 vaccines?
narrative: The user wants to find trustworthy, peer‑reviewed studies or official health agency reports that directly refute specific vaccine myths (e.g., micro‑chips, fertility risks). Relevance is judged by the source’s credibility, the clarity of the refutation, and the citation of reputable evidence.

title: COVID Travel Restrictions
description: What are the current COVID‑19 travel restrictions for traveling from France to Iceland during the winter season?
narrative: The user is planning a trip abroad and needs up‑to‑date, official information on COVID‑19 related travel restrictions for that specific itinerary. Relevant documents should provide current testing, quarantine, and vaccination requirements that apply to travelers from France heading to Iceland, including any temporary changes for the winter period.

title: Quantum Entanglement
description: What are the latest experimental techniques demonstrating quantum entanglement in high‑energy physics?
narrative: The user is a physics graduate student aiming to locate recent peer‑reviewed journal articles, conference proceedings, and technical reports that detail experimental methods for observing and manipulating quantum entanglement in high‑energy particle collisions (e.g., at the LHC or RHIC). Documents must be published after 2018, provide in‑depth methodological descriptions—including detector setups, measurement protocols, and statistical analyses—and focus specifically on high‑energy experimental contexts rather than theoretical or low‑energy systems.

title: Remote Work Stress
description: What are the psychological effects of prolonged remote working environments on employees?
narrative: The user seeks current research, empirical studies, and expert analyses that examine how extended periods of working from home influence mental health, stress levels, burnout, and work‑life balance, especially in the context of evolving organizational policies and technology use.

title: Hybrid Child Seat Installation
description: How do I correctly install a child car seat in a 2024 Hyundai Ioniq 5 hybrid with ISOFIX without compromising safety?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed, vehicle‑specific guidance on placing a child car seat in a newly equipped hybrid car. They need to know which ISOFIX anchors are compatible with the Ioniq 5, how to secure the seat to meet ECE R44/04 or i‑Size standards, and what weight or age limits still require a rear‑facing position. Additionally, they are interested in safety checks that should be performed after each installation, any known issues with the car’s rear‑view camera or seat belts, and best practices for maintaining the installation over time. The answer must address these vehicle‑specific criteria and explain how the user can verify that the seat is properly locked and that any electronic sensors are not inadvertently triggered.

title: Remote Work Housing
description: Where can a full‑time remote digital nomad find short‑term, furnished housing in major European tech hubs?
narrative: A remote employee seeks flexible, safe accommodations with reliable internet and workspace in cities such as Berlin, Warsaw, and Barcelona; relevant documents should include listings, price ranges, lease terms, and community reviews.

title: Historic Military Stores
description: What were the historical military surplus shops that operated in France during the 19th and early 20th centuries and how did they influence local economies?
narrative: The user is researching the economic impact and evolution of military surplus retail operations in historic French towns. They expect contextual timelines, business models, ownership details, and socio-economic effects, rather than contemporary retail listings or promotions. Relevance criteria include archival records, period trade publications, local economic studies, and scholarly analyses that discuss the role and legacy of these shops in their communities.

title: Solar Lease vs Buy
description: What are the financial, contractual, and incentive differences between leasing solar power systems for a residence versus buying them outright?
narrative: The user wants a detailed side‑by‑side comparison of total cost of ownership, tariff structures, lease terms, available tax credits, depreciation treatment, maintenance responsibilities, and potential resale value for residential solar installations.   Relevant documents should include state and local incentive programs, recent policy updates, case studies, and financial analyses that cover both low‑income and high‑income households.

title: Remote Work Office Utilization
description: How has the rise of remote work affected the utilization of office spaces in large metropolitan companies over the past five years?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based analysis of office space usage changes attributable to increased remote work, focusing on large companies (10k+ employees) in major U.S. and EU metros from 2018‑2023. Relevant sources include empirical studies, data reports, and industry analyses that quantify desk occupancy rates, meeting room demand, and flexible workspace adoption; policy briefs on real estate strategy; and longitudinal surveys that capture trends before and after the pandemic. Non‑scientific news accounts, anecdotal blogs, and documents focusing solely on small businesses or non‑English language studies are considered irrelevant. Documents should provide clear metrics, methodology, and discuss implications for corporate real‑estate planning.}

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Monitoring
description: What is the current evidence on the effectiveness of remote retinal imaging for early detection of diabetic retinopathy in primary care settings?
narrative: The user seeks recent systematic reviews or randomized controlled trials comparing teleophthalmology to in‑person screening, focusing on sensitivity, specificity, cost‑effectiveness, and patient adherence. Documents are relevant if they report empirical data or meta‑analysis from 2015 onwards; opinions, case studies without quantitative outcomes, or studies outside this time frame are considered less relevant.

title: Vegan Gluten‑Free Leek Pie
description: What are suitable recipes for a leek pie that is vegan, gluten‑free, and low sodium?
narrative: The user is preparing a meal for someone with diabetes and celiac disease. They need a recipe that excludes dairy, gluten, and limits sodium. Relevance should include ingredient lists, alternative flours or binding agents, salt substitutes, and detailed cooking instructions.

title: Urban Drone Delivery
description: I want to understand the legal, environmental, and logistical challenges of implementing drone delivery services in densely populated city centers.
narrative: The user is a city planning officer exploring the feasibility of drone delivery. Relevant documents should discuss regulations, case studies, impact assessments, air‑traffic coordination, privacy concerns, and environmental sustainability of drone operations in urban settings.

title: UNESCO Digital Heritage Europe
description: I want detailed information on UNESCO-supported digital heritage projects in Europe, including project scopes, funding amounts, timelines, and participating cultural institutions.
narrative: The user is a researcher preparing a comparative study of digital heritage initiatives across Europe. They need reliable documentation that lists each UNESCO-funded project, the specific cultural or archival objectives, the total budget and funding agency sources, the expected and actual launch dates, and the names and roles of partner institutions (e.g., museums, archives, universities). Relevance criteria: documents must provide explicit details on European UNESCO digital heritage projects with verifiable funding, institutional participation, and project milestones; generic digital heritage overviews or non-UNESCO initiatives are not considered relevant.

title: International Student Loans
description: What are the best long‑term loan options for international students pursuing a master's degree?
narrative: The user is planning to finance a graduate program abroad and wants to compare loan terms, interest rates, repayment plans, and eligibility criteria for various lenders, focusing on institutions that offer favorable conditions for non‑US citizens.

title: Oil Lamp Fuels
description: What fuels are appropriate and safe for use in a traditional oil lamp?
narrative: A hobbyist is researching which liquids—such as kerosene, alcohol, white spirit, or specialty lampant oils—can be safely burned in a classic oil lamp while minimizing soot, smoke, and fire risk. Relevance is judged by coverage of historical fuel usage, safety guidelines, combustion characteristics, and practical tips for lamp maintenance.

title: WWII Submarine Disappearances
description: Where did the Japanese submarine I‑19 vanish during the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands?
narrative: The user seeks primary and secondary documents—operational logs, wreckage reports, and contemporaneous naval dispatches—that specify the last known position and circumstances of I‑19’s disappearance, preferring sources that provide verifiable coordinates and corroborating evidence rather than general WWII overviews.

title: Quantum Entanglement in Neural Networks
description: How can quantum entanglement principles be applied to improve learning in artificial neural networks?
narrative: The user is researching advanced computational models where quantum mechanics may enhance neural network performance. Relevant documents should discuss both theoretical foundations of quantum entanglement and practical implementations or simulations in machine learning contexts, but should exclude purely biological or classical computational references.

title: SSA Appeal Process
description: What steps can I take to appeal a denial of the Special Solidarity Allowance due to insufficient work history, and what supporting documents are best?
narrative: The user wants detailed guidance on challenging an SSA refusal that cites a lack of sufficient employment duration. They need to know the legal framework and time limits for appeals, the exact sequence of actions to submit a written challenge, and the types of evidence—such as employment contracts, payroll records, freelance invoices, and tax filings—that are most persuasive. Relevance is determined by documents that outline official appeal procedures, relevant legal provisions (e.g., Article 38 of the Unemployment Insurance Convention), case law or historical examples of successful appeals, and practical tips for preparing a compelling case.
